title: platform-user — PHP attribute contracts
description: One attribute. `#[AsPlatformUserListener]` marks a class as a subscriber to PlatformUser-lifecycle events (hired, activated, suspended, offboarded, restored, manager-reassigned, department-changed, profile-updated). Discovered at boot by `PlatformUserServiceProvider` so internal services under `access/rbac`, `access/delegation`, `notifications`, `observability`, and the future on-call `escalation` module react without hard-coded event bindings.

attributes.name: AsPlatformUserListener

params.name: events
params.description: Which PlatformUser events this class reacts to. Wildcard `*` = every event under `Stackra\PlatformUser\Events\*`. Specific list e.g. `['PlatformUserHired', 'PlatformUserOffboarded']` subscribes to those only. Validated against the events map at boot; typos abort application boot.

params.name: queue
params.description: Queue name for asynchronous dispatch. Set to `null` for synchronous listeners (rare — reserve for audit tap-outs that MUST land in the same request cycle). Common values: `notifications` (default), `notifications-critical` (offboarding + suspension events which must not queue behind a backlog), `audit` (write-heavy compliance streams).

params.name: priority
params.description: Listener execution order. Lower values run first. Reserve 1–99 for framework-owned listeners (audit stamping, session revocation) that MUST run before user-defined listeners. Example: `RevokeTokensOnOffboard` runs at priority=10 so audit rows land first, and mail notifications run at 100 so revocation is already visible in the audit trail when they fire.

params.name: requires_platform_permission
params.description: Optional permission gate on the `platform_admin` guard. When set, the listener refuses to run if the triggering actor lacks the permission. Used by the `platform-user:audit-report` command's downstream listeners so a super_admin's report generation does not leak into a broader distribution list.
